data.title: FirstCash Closes $750 Million 6.125% Senior Notes Due 2034 Under New Indenture
data.description:
FirstCash completed a private offering of $750 million of 6.125% senior notes due 2034, issued by subsidiary FirstCash. The unsecured notes are guaranteed by the company and certain domestic subsidiaries, accrue interest from May 1, 2026, and pay semi-annually each May 1 and November 1 starting November 1, 2026. The Indenture includes customary covenants and redemption provisions, including a make-whole call before May 1, 2029 and optional redemptions thereafter. The company expects the transaction to enhance liquidity and financial flexibility.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Indenture governing $750 million 6.125% senior notes due 2034
  • Counterparty: BOKF, as trustee; Jefferies, as representative of initial purchasers
  • Signed / Effective: May 01 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 8 years, maturing May 1 2034
  • Reason: Enhance liquidity and financial flexibility

Original SEC Filing: FirstCash Holdings, Inc. [ FCFS ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: First Western Financial shares investor presentation detailing growth, 1Q26 results and $3.24B assets
data.description:
First Western Financial furnished an investor presentation highlighting strong 1Q26 operating momentum, growth initiatives, and key financial metrics including $3.24 billion in assets.

Key Highlights:

  • Filed investor presentation under Regulation FD to be used with investors and analysts during FY2026.
  • Reported assets of $3.24B, loans $2.69B, deposits $2.84B and AUM $7.24B as of 3/31/2026.
  • 1Q26 net income $6.2M ($0.63 diluted EPS); diluted EPS rose 85% QoQ and tangible book value $24.87/share.
  • Total deposits rose 3.5% QoQ (+$95M) and loans held for investment increased $41M in 1Q26.
  • Net interest margin improved to 2.81% in 1Q26; efficiency ratio improved to 73.11% for the quarter.

Original SEC Filing: First Western Financial Inc [ MYFW ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

data.title: Borealis Foods Secures $17M Term Loan From Oxus, Sets $33.3M Conversion; Retires Frontwell Debt
data.description:
Borealis Foods secured a $17.0 million secured term loan from Oxus Capital at 12% interest, maturing Apr 27, 2031, to refinance debt and support liquidity. The company used proceeds to repay about $16.2 million outstanding under its Frontwell facility, which was terminated with no prepayment fee and all liens released. Separately, Borealis entered a conversion agreement covering $33.3 million of shareholder debt that will automatically convert to equity at the 20-day VWAP if a $70 million equity raise at $9.00 per share is not completed by Jul 1, 2026. The financing package extends runway, simplifies cash controls, and provides a path to deleveraging.

Agreement 1: Borealis Foods Secures $17 Million Term Loan From Oxus Capital, Refinancing Frontwell Debt

  • Agreement type: Secured term loan credit agreement
  • Counterparty: Oxus Capital
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 27 2026 / Apr 27 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Apr 27 2031
  • Reason: Refinance Frontwell facility and add liquidity

Agreement 2: Borealis Foods Sets Debt-to-Equity Conversion Pact on $33.3 Million If $70M Raise Fails

  • Agreement type: Debt-to-equity conversion agreement
  • Counterparty: Oxus Capital; Reza Soltanzadeh; Barthelemy Helg
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 27 2026 / Apr 27 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Jul 01 2026
  • Reason: Reduce leverage if equity raise falls short

Agreement 3: Borealis Foods Ends Frontwell Credit Facility After $16.2 Million Payoff; No Prepayment Fee

  • Agreement terminated: Amended and Restated Credit Agreement (term loan and revolver)
  • Counterparty: Frontwell Capital Partners
  • Original agreement date: Aug 10 2023
  • Termination date: Apr 27 2026
  • Termination type: Early
  • Exit fees / payments: None
  • Reason: Refreshed with new Oxus term loan

Original SEC Filing: Borealis Foods Inc. [ BRLS ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: InspireMD Withdraws Guidance, Recalls CGuard Prime
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

InspireMD is voluntarily recalling its CGuard Prime 135 cm carotid stent delivery system after determining that the system did not meet performance expectations.

The medical technology company said Friday that the voluntary recall came after the stent system for the prevention of stroke did not meet expectations during carotid artery stenting procedures.

The company is implementing design improvements to the CGuard Prime stent delivery system and expects approval from the Food and Drug Administration by the first half of 2027, the company said.

The company is withdrawing its full-year revenue guidance.

Write to Christopher Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com

data.title: S&P, Nasdaq close at records as Amgen weighs on Dow
data.description:
Key points:
  • Nasdaq rallies~0.9%, S&P 500 gains ~0.3%, Dow dips ~0.3%
  • Tech, Cons Disc only S&P sector gainers; Energy weakest group
  • Dollar rises slightly; bitcoin rallies >2%; gold slips; US crude off >2%
  • US 10-year Treasury yield edges down to ~4.38%

S&P, NASDAQ CLOSE AT RECORDS AS AMGEN WEIGHS ON DOW

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq rose on Friday to close out the trading week at record levels, after recording their best monthly performance since 2020 in April, buttressed by strong earnings and renewed hopes that peace talks between the U.S. and Iran could restart.

Tech rallied more than 1% as the best-performing S&P 500 sector, powered by a gain of about 3% in Apple , which was the biggest boost to each of the three major indexes after the iPhone maker's quarterly earnings.

Earnings have helped stocks rally in recent weeks in the face of broken-down talks between the U.S. and Iran, and first-quarter earnings growth is now tracking at 27.8%, according to LSEG data, up from the 16.1% a week ago and 14.1% at the start of April.

Amgen shares were off about 5%, to weigh heavily on the Dow, after reporting quarterly earnings as the biotech company disclosed during its earnings call that it received a notice of proposed adjustment from the U.S. Internal Revenue Service (IRS) for tax years 2016 to 2018.

Energy fell 1.3% as the worst-performing S&P sector, as both Exxon Mobil and Chevron shares were lower after their quarterly results, while crude prices dropped after Iran proposed new peace talks, although U.S. President Donald Trump said he was not satisfied with the latest Iranian proposal.

For the week, the S&P 500 rose 0.9%, the Dow advanced 0.5%, and the Nasdaq gained 1.1%. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq secured their sixth straight week of gains, their longest since October 2024.

Below is your closing market snapshot:

data.title: Weyerhaeuser Co Q1 2026: Revenue $1.73B, EPS $0.22— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Weyerhaeuser Co reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $1.73B, down slightly from $1.76B a year earlier, while net income and diluted EPS rose meaningfully to $156M and $0.22, respectively.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $1,727M for Q1 2026, down from $1,763M in the year-ago quarter (2% decline).
  • Net income: $156M for Q1 2026, up from $83M in the year-ago quarter (88% increase).
  • Diluted earnings per share: $0.22 for Q1 2026, up from $0.11 in the year-ago quarter (100% increase).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix shifted as Strategic Land Solutions sales rose sharply, driven by a $94M conservation easement that boosted segment revenue versus prior year.
  • Wood Products demand softened: overall Wood Products sales fell roughly 10%, pressured by lower realizations in OSB and lumber and modest volume declines.
  • Timberlands: western log realizations and volumes declined, but timberland operating income increased due to a $58M gain on a timberland sale.
  • Operational investments: capital spending continued, including construction of the Monticello TimberStrand facility to add about 10M cubic feet of capacity, on track for a 2027 start.

Original SEC Filing: WEYERHAEUSER CO [ WY ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Rithm Property Trust Inc. 1Q 2026: Net loss $(3.28)M, EPS $(0.43) — 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Rithm Property Trust Inc. reported results for the first quarter of 2026, posting a net loss attributable to common stockholders of $(3.28)M, an improvement from a $(3.74)M loss in the year-ago quarter. Diluted loss per share narrowed to $(0.43) from $(0.49) in 1Q 2025. The filing did not present a standalone revenue line in Part I, Item 1–2 for the quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: Not reported for the quarter (no explicit “Revenue”/Sales line in Part I, Item 1–2 for Q1 2026).
  • Net income: Net loss attributable to common stockholders $(3.28)M for Q1 2026 vs. net loss $(3.74)M in Q1 2025 (improvement of $0.46M YoY).
  • Diluted EPS: $(0.43) for Q1 2026 vs. $(0.49) in Q1 2025 (improved by $0.06 YoY).

Business Highlights

  • Portfolio focus shifted toward commercial CRE investments while continuing to manage a legacy residential mortgage portfolio and RMBS positions.
  • Generated significant investing cash through CMBS sales and loan collections; maintained approximately $96M in cash to support operations and capital deployment.
  • G&A and professional fees rose due to transaction activity and evaluation of a potential equity raise.
  • Strategy emphasizes flexible CRE lending across senior, mezzanine, preferred equity and CMBS to capture dislocations in selective credit markets.

Original SEC Filing: Rithm Property Trust Inc. [ RPT ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

data.title: REPUBLIC AIRWAYS HOLDINGS INC. 1Q 2026: Revenue $527.4M, EPS $0.58— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Republic Airways Holdings Inc. reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue up 33.6% year-over-year to $527.4M while net income was flat at $26.9M and diluted EPS declined to $0.58 from $0.68 in the prior-year quarter.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth was driven by a 30.4% increase in block hours and higher departures versus the prior year.
  • Republic operated exclusively under partner brands with flying split roughly 37% American, 21% Delta and 42% United by departures.

  • Average daily utilization remained about 9.6 hours per aircraft; integration costs from the Mesa merger continue and an Aviation Campus training center is coming online.

data.title: Gates Industrial Corp plc 1Q 2026: Revenue $851.1M, EPS $0.23— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Gates Industrial Corp plc reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $851.1M and diluted EPS of $0.23, roughly flat revenue versus the year-ago quarter and a slight decline in earnings.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$851.1M$847.6M0.4%
Net income²$66.2M$68.3M(3.1%)
Diluted EPS³$0.23$0.24(4.2%)

Business Highlights

  • Net sales rose modestly 0.4% to $851.1M; on a core basis (excluding currency) sales declined about 2.9%.
  • Aftermarket represented roughly 68% of sales, with OEM at 32%; personal mobility and industrial OEM end markets showed relative strength.
  • Gates brand maintained premium positioning supporting recurring, higher-margin aftermarket demand and technology reputation.
  • Management is focused on material cost savings, footprint optimization and productivity; one-time footprint, restructuring and system costs are expected in H1 2026.
  • By segment, Power Transmission sales were up 1.1% (core -2.5%) while Fluid Power sales fell 0.8% (core -3.5%), with margins pressured by lower volumes.

data.description:
FTAI Aviation Ltd. reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue rising to $830.7M and diluted EPS of $1.29, driven by stronger product sales and expanded servicing activity versus the year-ago quarter.

  • The company continued a strategic shift toward an asset-light servicer model and launched the Strategic Capital Initiative; a 2025 partnership raised $2.0B and became the primary buyer of on-lease 737NG/A320ceo assets.
  • FTAI expanded in-house manufacturing and MRO capacity through acquisitions (LMCES, Pac Aero, ATOPS) and internalized management of compressor repairs and related services.

data.title: BERKLEY W R CORP 1Q 2026: Revenue $3.69B, EPS $1.31— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
BERKLEY W R CORP reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $3.69B, net income to common stockholders of $515.2M and diluted EPS of $1.31, each up versus the year-ago quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue was $3.69B, compared with $3.547B in the prior-year quarter; YoY change 4.1%.
  • Net income was $515.2M, compared with $417.6M in the prior-year quarter; YoY change 23.4%.
  • Diluted EPS was $1.31, compared with $1.04 in the prior-year quarter; YoY change 26%.

Business Highlights

  • Net premiums earned rose 3% year over year to $3.12B, driven by higher Insurance segment premiums and rate increases.
  • Insurance premiums increased 5%, led by short‑tail, auto and professional lines, while reinsurance premiums declined 9%.
  • Renewal retention was about 81% with average renewal pricing up ~6.6% (7.2% excluding workers’ comp), indicating solid pricing power.
  • GAAP combined ratio improved slightly to 90.7%, supported by underwriting gains in Reinsurance and Monoline Excess.
  • Investment income rose 12%, reflecting a larger fixed‑maturity portfolio and stronger returns from investment funds.

Original SEC Filing: BERKLEY W R CORP [ WRB ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

data.title: CBOT soybeans end higher; soyoil futures set fresh contract highs
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade soybean futures ended higher on Friday on a bear spread, even as the most-active July contract (SN26) stayed inside Thursday's trading range, market analysts said.

  • CBOT July soybeans (SN26) settled up 7-3/4 cents at $12.03-1/4 a bushel.

  • Soybean futures drew support as soyoil futures (0#BO:) set fresh contract highs, buoyed by demand for biodiesel.

  • The most-active July soyoil (BON26) settled 0.62 cent higher at 75.16 cents per pound.

  • Crush margins also gave soybean futures a boost. The Chicago Board of Trade July (SMSIN26-BON26-SN26) board crush, one indication of soy processors' profit margins, was last up 3 cents at $3.29 per bushel of soybeans.

  • July soymeal (SMN26) ended 40 cents higher at $319.30 per short ton.

  • Weather concerns were also at play in the corn and soybean markets on Friday, traders said.

  • Some brokers and market analysts said that their farmer clients in central and eastern parts of the U.S. Corn Belt had not yet started planting, either because it had been too wet or too cold.

  • Global oil prices turned lower on Friday after hitting a four-year high of more than $126 a barrel earlier on Thursday on concerns the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran could lead to a protracted Middle East supply disruption that could inflict deeper damage on the global economy.

  • Lower crude oil prices can put pressure on the soy complex, as soybeans are a common feedstock for biofuel.

data.title: Dow slips 152 pts as S&P 500 hits record high, Apple lifts Nasdaq
data.description:
US equities ended Friday on a mixed note, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite extending their record-setting run, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average lagged as investors weighed strong earnings against geopolitical uncertainty and easing oil prices.

The S&P 500 rose 0.3% to a fresh intraday and closing high, while the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.9%, also reaching a new record.

In contrast, the Dow fell 153 points, or 0.3%, underperforming its peers.

The latest moves cap a strong period for equities, with both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq posting their strongest monthly gains since 2020.

The Dow also recorded its best monthly performance since November 2024, underscoring the broader strength in markets despite ongoing global risks.

Apple leads tech rally, lifts broader market

Gains in technology stocks were led by Apple, which climbed more than 3% after delivering better-than-expected fiscal second-quarter earnings and revenue.

The company also issued a stronger-than-expected outlook for the current quarter, helping offset concerns over weaker iPhone sales, which missed estimates for the second time in three quarters.

Apple’s performance provided a significant boost to the broader market, particularly the Nasdaq, as investors continued to reward companies showing resilience in earnings and forward guidance.

The strength in tech was echoed across other major names.

Several companies within the so-called “Magnificent Seven” reported earnings during the week, with investors closely monitoring whether heavy artificial intelligence investments are beginning to translate into returns.

Beyond mega-cap tech, corporate earnings more broadly have surprised to the upside.

According to LSEG data, analysts now expect first-quarter earnings growth of 27.8% year-on-year, marking the strongest expansion since the fourth quarter of 2021.

Of the 314 companies that have reported so far, 83% have beaten earnings estimates, while 78% have exceeded revenue forecasts.

Oil prices ease amid Iran developments

Oil prices declined during the session, providing additional support to equities, as markets reacted to signs of renewed diplomatic engagement between the United States and Iran.

US West Texas Intermediate crude futures fell 2.98% to settle at $101.94 per barrel, while Brent crude dropped 2.02% to $108.17.

Prices had earlier fallen further before paring losses after comments from President Donald Trump.

The decline followed reports that Iran had submitted a response through Pakistani mediators to a US proposal aimed at ending the ongoing conflict.

However, Trump later expressed dissatisfaction with the offer, stating that Iran “wants to make a deal, but I’m not satisfied with it.”

The conflict has disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy corridor, contributing to volatility in oil markets and raising concerns about inflation and global supply chains.

Strong earnings and outlook support equities

Despite geopolitical headwinds, the broader outlook for equities remains constructive, supported by robust earnings growth and improving investor sentiment.

Economic data released during the week showed US factory activity expanding for a fourth consecutive month, although inflation pressures remain a concern, with the prices-paid component reaching its highest level in four years.

As markets enter May, a period historically associated with weaker returns, investors are weighing seasonal trends against current momentum.

Since 1945, the S&P 500 has averaged gains of about 2% from May through October, compared with around 7% from November through April.

data.title: Intel clears regulatory hurdle for AI partnership
data.description:
Intel Corporation just got a bit of regulatory relief, clearing the way to move ahead with its partnership with SambaNova Systems without delay.

The FTC granted early termination on the review tied to Intel's involvement in SambaNova's $350 million funding round, which basically means the deal doesn't have to sit through a longer waiting period. Along with investing, Intel is also teaming up with the startup more closely as it looks to strengthen its position in the AI space.

SambaNova is using that funding to scale up manufacturing and cloud capacity, and it's already lining up customers. The company said SoftBank (SFTBY) will be the first to deploy its new SN50 systems in AI data centers in Japan, with the hardware promising a big jump in performance compared to the previous generation.

data.title: WY: Q1 2026 net earnings surged 88% on timberland gains and insurance recovery, despite lower sales
data.description:
Net earnings for Q1 2026 nearly doubled year-over-year, driven by gains on timberland sales and insurance recoveries, despite a 2% decline in net sales. Strategic Land Solutions revenue surged, while Wood Products and Timberlands saw lower sales. Liquidity and capital resources remain strong.

Original document: Weyerhaeuser Company [WY] SEC 10-Q Quarterly Report — May. 1 2026

data.title: Tesla generates over $500 Million from Musk-linked companies
data.description:
Tesla is doing more business within Elon Musk's own network than it might seem at first glance, with new filings showing over $500 million in revenue last year tied to his other companies.

A big chunk of that came from xAI, which accounted for about $430.1 million, mostly through purchases of Tesla's Megapack energy systems. Another $143.3 million came from SpaceX, largely tied to vehicle sales. That SpaceX figure is new, it wasn't included in Tesla's original filing earlier this year and only showed up in the updated version.

And this isn't slowing down. Tesla has already booked around $78.1 million in revenue from xAI in the first part of 2026, with smaller contributions from SpaceX. At the same time, the links between these businesses are getting tighter. Tesla is starting to integrate xAI's Grok chatbot into its vehicles, and there are broader plans around working together on chip development and infrastructure.
